model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: This matchup projects as close on baseline team strength, but Louisville’s biggest swing factor remains Mikel Brown Jr.’s back injury—reporting indicates Louisville’s results dipped in games he missed, and any limitation raises their turnover and late-clock shot-creation risk against Michigan State’s physical, disciplined style. Louisville’s efficiency profile is strong enough that it can absolutely win if Brown is near full strength and the Cardinals make shots in the half court, but the health uncertainty makes Michigan State the slightly safer advance. Because Louisville has a very real path if Brown looks right, confidence stays modest.

reasoningStep.title: Availability-driven tiebreaker in a near coin-flip
reasoningStep.summary: With Louisville and Michigan State looking like similar-caliber teams, I leaned to the side with fewer known availability concerns and used Louisville’s point-guard health uncertainty as the main separator.
evidence: Louisville ratings snapshot: KenPom #18 (AdjEM 25.42; AdjO 124.1 #20; AdjD 98.7), Torvik #16 (AdjO rank 23; AdjD rank 23).
USA TODAY Sportsbook Wire (3/15/2026) notes Louisville went 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury and stresses needing him healthy for a deep run.
Matchup logic: if Brown is limited, Louisville’s ball security and half-court creation become more fragile, which increases upset risk against Michigan State’s physical defense and depth.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr. for March Madness seed Skip to main content Home NFL MLB NBA NHL NCAA Football NCAA Basketball UFC/MMA PGA Tour Games WNBA Newsletter Soccer NASCAR CFL Bet 101 Industry News Betting Map Online Casino USA TODAY Sports Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr.'s injury for March Madness seed C.L. Brown Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament and gives an update on his recovery for the NCAA Tournament. Louisville basketball received a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. The Cardinals rated well in many key metrics, but they went just 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury. UofL will need its star point guard healthy to navigate its challenging path for a deep March Madness run. Louisville basketball , in a season of many what-ifs, just had another on Selection Sunday with the NCAA’s release of the tournament bracket . The Cardinals received a No. 6 seed in the East Region and will face No. 11-seed and American Conference champion South Florida on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. in the first round. That’s at least one seed line higher than they should be. As much as the fate of how long they last in the nation’s best championship tournament will be determined by the availability of star freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. , their position as a 6-seed can be attributed to him too. Through no fault of his own, the back injury that forced Brown to miss 10 regular-season games and kept him sidelined for the entire ACC Tournament cost UofL from having a better seed and a slightly easier path to advancing. Brown has done everything he can to play, even trying to play hurt through the pain. After he did so in UofL's loss to Clemson, coach Pat Kelsey said it was best to shut him down until he could come back fully healthy. Brown told The Courier Journal after Louisville's ACC Tournament win over SMU that it's been a childhood dream to play in the NCAA Tournament, which he called "the best basketball (atmosphere) in the world." "I'm not going to just sit out for no reason," Brown said. "I want to play with these guys, I want to be out there, I want to play for the team, but my health is first." Brown's absence was costly in terms of the hypothetical games the Cards perhaps could have won had he played. And it was costly in terms of the reality that his status was a topic of conversation by the NCAA Selection Committee when it began constructing the bracket. Brown, who is by all accounts going to be a top-10 NBA draft pick in June , was kept out of playing in Charlotte for the sole purpose of getting him healthy enough to play in the NCAAs. “Obviously, he’s one of the premier players in the country, and so Louisville playing without him certainly is a different team,” said Sun Belt Commissioner Keith Gill, who served as this year's

Their defense has been so suffocating that the gap between No. 1 and No. 2 on that side was once bigger than any in KenPom history.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
